sections.biography-source-undefined: Hu Qian, courtesy name Gongxing, hailed from Wei commandery. How he ended up in Yi province was not recorded. Although he was not extensive in knowledge, he was outstanding and distinguished for his strong memory. With regards to the ceremonies of the ancestral system and determining what type of the five types of mourning attire was appropriate for mourning, he could draw them on the ground with his fingers and palms, as if grasping them with a raise of his hands. For those qualities, Liu Bei appointed him as Academician. Liu Bei had just taken over Yi province and wanted to reform and revitalize education by organizing ancient texts. Hu Qian worked alongside Xu Ci, Lai Min, and Meng Guang. However, there were many disagreements on how to implement the reforms. Hu Qian developed a hostile rivalry with Xu Ci, engaging in mutual arguments and attacks. They would defame and slander with rage and anger, evident in their tone and expression. For books and tomes that they had or lacked, they would not borrow from each other. From time to time, they would use flogging canes in order to intimidate and subdue each other. Their arrogance in themselves and envy of others had culminated in such. Liu Bei felt sorry that this was how they were. When he held a major meeting of the officials, he ordered performers to look hostile like the two and mimic their fierce quarrels. With drinking into inebriation and entertainment being conducted, it was amusing. In the beginning, they challenged each other over phrases and meanings, and in the conclusion, they compelled each other through blades and staffs. This was meant to stir and rebuke them. Hu Qian died before Xu Ci.

full_text:
Hu Qian (Gongxing) 胡潛 (公興)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Hu Qian (胡潛), styledGongxing (公興), was born in Wei commandery. Meant to work on establishing a procedure for Customs in the area. Instead spent time fighting Xu Ci. Hu Qian was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom.

BiographySource Undefined
Hu Qian, courtesy name Gongxing, hailed from Wei commandery. How he ended up in Yi province was not recorded. Although he was not extensive in knowledge, he was outstanding and distinguished for his strong memory. With regards to the ceremonies of the ancestral system and determining what type of the five types of mourning attire was appropriate for mourning, he could draw them on the ground with his fingers and palms, as if grasping them with a raise of his hands. For those qualities, Liu Bei appointed him as Academician. Liu Bei had just taken over Yi province and wanted to reform and revitalize education by organizing ancient texts. Hu Qian worked alongside Xu Ci, Lai Min, and Meng Guang. However, there were many disagreements on how to implement the reforms. Hu Qian developed a hostile rivalry with Xu Ci, engaging in mutual arguments and attacks. They would defame and slander with rage and anger, evident in their tone and expression. For books and tomes that they had or lacked, they would not borrow from each other. From time to time, they would use flogging canes in order to intimidate and subdue each other. Their arrogance in themselves and envy of others had culminated in such. Liu Bei felt sorry that this was how they were. When he held a major meeting of the officials, he ordered performers to look hostile like the two and mimic their fierce quarrels. With drinking into inebriation and entertainment being conducted, it was amusing. In the beginning, they challenged each other over phrases and meanings, and in the conclusion, they compelled each other through blades and staffs. This was meant to stir and rebuke them. Hu Qian died before Xu Ci.
